title: Optimal Cue Combination: The Statistician's Frog

runtime.expected_outcome: When both channels provide independent noisy estimates, the posterior is the precision-weighted average -- exactly the optimal-cue-combination result from psychophysics.

audiences.kid: If both eyes and ears give you guesses, the best answer is a weighted average -- trust each more if it's clearer.
audiences.real: Gaussian factored likelihoods produce the optimal-cue-combination posterior: precision-weighted mean, summed precisions.
audiences.equation: In a Gaussian multi-modal bundle, closed-form posterior has mu = (pi_1 mu_1 + pi_2 mu_2)/(pi_1 + pi_2), pi = pi_1 + pi_2.
audiences.derivation: Multiplying two Gaussian densities yields another Gaussian with summed precisions and precision-weighted mean. This reproduces Ernst & Banks (2002) haptic-visual integration in closed form.

orchestrate.objective: Reproduce optimal-cue-combination as a special case of multi-modal Active Inference.
orchestrate.context: frog_pond with two channels whose precisions you sweep; posterior is the precision-weighted average.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 5.
Ernst & Banks (2002), Humans integrate visual and haptic information in a statistically optimal fashion.
